data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on MapLight Therapeutics, Reaffirms $45 Price Target Ahead of Key Phase 2 ZEPHYR Readout
data.description:
Analyst Francois Brisebois of LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on MapLight Therapeutics, Inc., retaining the price target of $45.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Francois Brisebois has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably the upcoming Phase 2 ZEPHYR readout for ML-007C-MA in schizophrenia and the drug’s perceived commercial opportunity. He views the planned KOL event with leading schizophrenia experts as an important validation step, underscoring credible interest in 007’s clinical profile and market positioning.

He also highlights MapLight Therapeutics’ solid funding runway, with roughly two years of cash based on current burn, which supports execution through key data milestones without immediate financing pressure. Against this backdrop, he maintains a favorable risk‑reward outlook and keeps his $45 price target unchanged, reflecting confidence that positive Phase 2 data could meaningfully unlock value despite development and market risks.

According to TipRanks, Brisebois is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 5.9% and a 41.95% success rate. Brisebois covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Praxis Precision Medicines, Savara, and Definium Therapeutics.

In another report released on June 25, Raymond James also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $46.00 price target.

data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Solid Core Operations and Temporary Headwinds; $30 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Alexander Paris, an analyst from Barrington, reiterated the Buy rating on Franklin Covey Company. The associated price target remains the same with $30.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Alexander Paris has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, despite the modest guidance reduction for fiscal 2026. He notes that third-quarter results were broadly consistent with expectations, with revenue growth and improved EBITDA margins supported by disciplined cost control, indicating that the core operations remain solid even as reported figures came in slightly below prior forecasts.

Paris emphasizes that the guidance cut stems from specific, largely temporary issues—including contract timing, a budget-driven adjustment at an education client, and geopolitical pressures—rather than a deterioration in underlying demand. He also highlights that FC shares still trade at a discount to historical EBITDA multiples, and he anticipates a return to revenue and profit growth in fiscal 2027, which underpins his unchanged $30 price target and continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Roth MKM also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $25.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Kroger’s $1.65bn Giant Eagle Deal Seen as Margin‑Stable, EPS‑Accretive Growth Catalyst Supporting Buy Rating
data.description:
Kroger Company, the Consumer Defensive sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Greg Badishkanian from Wolfe Research maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $86.00 price target.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Greg Badishkanian has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Kroger’s acquisition of Giant Eagle and its broader strategic posture. He views the $1.65bn purchase of a $9bn‑sales business as financially attractive, noting that EBIT margins at Giant Eagle are comparable to Kroger’s existing portfolio and should support margin stability while adding scale in overlapping Midwestern markets.

He also highlights that the deal is structured to preserve Kroger’s leverage within its 2.3–2.5x net debt to adjusted EBITDA target range, allowing the company to continue its share repurchase and dividend programs. With an expected 6% boost to 2027 sales, an estimated $200–250mm incremental EBIT, and low‑single‑digit EPS accretion in year two, he sees the transaction as evidence that management is pursuing more offensive growth, reinforcing the long‑term investment case for the stock.

Badishkanian covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Jack In The Box, Winnebago Industries, and Leslie’s. According to TipRanks, Badishkanian has an average return of 9.3% and a 54.55% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Evercore ISI also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $80.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Nigel Coe Reiterates Hold on MSC Industrial Amid Solid Q3 Results but Limited Upside Catalysts
data.description:
Wolfe Research analyst Nigel Coe has maintained their neutral stance on MSM stock, giving a Hold rating yesterday.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Nigel Coe has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to MSC Industrial’s solid but not exceptional performance. The company delivered healthy third-quarter sales growth, supported largely by pricing power and modest volume gains, while also expanding gross margin through favorable price-cost dynamics.

At the same time, operating margin improvement, though positive, remains incremental and partly influenced by one-time items such as litigation-related costs and recent headcount actions. Taken together, the results indicate a stable, well-managed business, but without a sufficiently compelling catalyst or valuation upside to justify a more aggressive rating than Hold.

Coe covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Johnson Controls, Madison Air Solutions Corp. Class A, and Otis Worldwide. According to TipRanks, Coe has an average return of 8.2% and a 54.29% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Tesla: Strong Q2 Deliveries Support Core Franchise but Valuation Keeps Risk/Reward Neutral, Warrants Hold
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jed Dorsheimer has maintained their neutral stance on TSLA stock, giving a Hold rating on June 26.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction TSLA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Jed Dorsheimer has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Tesla’s strong second-quarter deliveries meaningfully exceeding market expectations, driven largely by robust Model 3 and Model Y demand across key global regions. While this reinforces the durability of Tesla’s core auto franchise, it also sets a higher performance bar going forward, making it harder for future quarters to deliver comparable positive surprises.

At the same time, Jed notes that some legacy models, such as the Model S and Model X, are nearing the end of their demand cycle, suggesting that recent strength in these vehicles may not be sustainable. Balancing the solid near-term operational execution against potential normalization in growth and the already demanding valuation, he concludes that the risk/reward profile is currently neutral, warranting a Hold rather than a more aggressive rating.

In another report released on June 26, Morgan Stanley also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $415.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
